title: Logout Functionality Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of secure logout in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of a secure logout process?
options: To make the user's password shorter.
To ensure that the user's identity is verified again with a new session.
To terminate the user's session on both the client and the server side.
To clear the browser's cache.

questions.question: Which HTTP method should always be used for logout?
options: GET
POST
DELETE
PUT

questions.question: In a JWT-based system, why is deleting the token from localStorage alone not enough for a secure logout?
options: Because the token is stored in the browser's history.
Because the token remains valid and can be reused by an attacker until it expires.
Because the token is automatically regenerated by the server.
Because users can always copy the token before logging out.

questions.question: What is 'Blacklisting' in the context of logout?
options: A way to block specific user IP addresses.
Storing a logged-out token's ID in a cache like Redis so the server can refuse it for any subsequent requests.
Creating a list of common passwords that are not allowed.
Deleting a user from the entire application.

questions.question: What's the best practice for token storage in a high-security app?
options: Storing only the refresh token and generating access tokens on the fly.
Using both Access and Refresh tokens with short-lived access tokens and httpOnly cookies for the refresh token.
Storing tokens in the browser's local storage.
It's the only way to log in on a mobile device.
